Description: Measures how well methods in a class use the same fields. Low cohesion (high LCOM) indicates the class may have too many responsibilities.
Python Libraries / Source: Python: lcom, cohesion; Theoretical: Chidamber & Kemerer (1994)

Description: Number of classes to which a class is coupled. High coupling implies interdependence.
Python Libraries / Source: General static analysis; CK metrics suite

Description: Number of modules that depend on this one. High Ca indicates a stable, reused module.
Interpretation / Threshold: Higher can be good for core modules.

Description: Number of external modules this one depends on. High Ce = high dependency.
I = Ce / (Ca + Ce). Measures how prone a module is to change.
Ratio of abstract classes/interfaces to total classes in a module.
Interpretation / Threshold: A in [0,1]; ~0.5 ideal for balanced design.

Description: D = |A + I - 1|; deviation from ideal balance between abstraction and stability.
Number of linearly independent paths through a method.
Measures program vocabulary, length, volume, effort, and estimated bugs.
Sum of method complexities in a class; reflects overall class complexity.
Number of methods that can be invoked in response to a message to the class.
Maximum inheritance depth from root class to leaf.
Number of immediate subclasses.
Interpretation / Threshold: Varies by context; higher implies reuse, too high = fragility.

Description: Ratio of overridden methods to potential polymorphic methods.
Composite measure of modularity from size, complexity, cohesion, coupling.
Degree to which classes in a package are related.
Incoming/outgoing dependencies between packages.
Number of cycles in dependency graph.
Tracks violations of the ADP by detecting cycles.
Measures if dependencies point from unstable to stable modules.
Composite metric from CC, LOC, Halstead Volume, comment density.
Proportion of comment lines to total LOC.
Number of lines added/removed per commit or release.
Measures how often two modules change together over time.
Extent to which components modified together serve the same purpose.
How often changes occur in a component.
Composite indicator of single responsibility violations (large classes, low cohesion).
Ratio of unused interface methods to total methods used by a client.
Degree to which dependencies are on abstractions rather than concretes.
Measures ratio of extensions vs modifications needed for new behavior.
